title: Git Foundations: Evidence, Collaboration, and Recovery
description: Developers and technical collaborators who need Git understanding from first repository through safe team release and recovery. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate git snapshots, index, objects, branches, merges, remotes, conflicts, content policy, tags, revisions, undo, rebase, hooks, reviews, release, and recovery through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate safe simulated repository operations with status, diff, graph, ref, and recovery evidence through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate four cumulative repository projects through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

competencies.statement: Explain Git history as content-addressed snapshots connected by parent links and named references.
misconceptions: Git stores only line-by-line differences between every adjacent version.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain Git history as content-addressed snapshots connected by parent links and named references.

competencies.statement: Distinguish working tree, Git directory, common directory, bare repository, linked worktree, and nested repository boundaries.
misconceptions: Every .git path is necessarily a directory containing all repository data.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish working tree, Git directory, common directory, bare repository, linked worktree, and nested repository boundaries.

competencies.statement: Inspect system, global, local, worktree, command, and environment configuration with origin and scope evidence.
misconceptions: The nearest config file always overrides every command-line setting.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inspect system, global, local, worktree, command, and environment configuration with origin and scope evidence.

competencies.statement: Set and verify author identity, default branch, editor, pager, and line-ending policy without changing unrelated scopes.
misconceptions: GitHub login identity automatically configures every local Git author.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Set and verify author identity, default branch, editor, pager, and line-ending policy without changing unrelated scopes.

competencies.statement: Use installed help, version evidence, dry runs, status, and backups before following repository-changing instructions.
misconceptions: A command from a current web page behaves identically on every installed Git version.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use installed help, version evidence, dry runs, status, and backups before following repository-changing instructions.

competencies.statement: Trace HEAD, index, and working-tree versions of a path before using status, diff, add, restore, or commit.
misconceptions: git add sends files directly from the working tree into permanent history.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace HEAD, index, and working-tree versions of a path before using status, diff, add, restore, or commit.

competencies.statement: Read branch, staged, unstaged, untracked, rename, conflict, and ahead/behind status without treating short output as context-free.
misconceptions: A clean working tree proves the local branch matches its remote.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Read branch, staged, unstaged, untracked, rename, conflict, and ahead/behind status without treating short output as context-free.

competencies.statement: Select working-tree, staged, commit-to-commit, path-limited, word, and summary diffs from the review question.
misconceptions: git diff always shows every change that the next commit will contain.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Select working-tree, staged, commit-to-commit, path-limited, word, and summary diffs from the review question.

competencies.statement: Stage whole files or selected hunks while verifying the exact index diff and preserving unrelated work.
misconceptions: Interactive staging rewrites the working file to match selected hunks.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Stage whole files or selected hunks while verifying the exact index diff and preserving unrelated work.

competencies.statement: Create a cohesive commit with an imperative summary, rationale, test evidence, and no accidental generated or secret data.
misconceptions: A large commit is acceptable when its message lists every changed file.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Create a cohesive commit with an imperative summary, rationale, test evidence, and no accidental generated or secret data.

competencies.statement: Query commit graph topology, decorations, authorship, dates, messages, and changed paths with an intentional log format.
misconceptions: git log lists commits in a universal causal order.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Query commit graph topology, decorations, authorship, dates, messages, and changed paths with an intentional log format.

competencies.statement: Relate blobs, trees, commits, tags, object IDs, content identity, and repository reachability.
misconceptions: Renaming a file changes the stored blob bytes and therefore always creates a new blob.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Relate blobs, trees, commits, tags, object IDs, content identity, and repository reachability.

competencies.statement: Inspect commits, trees, blobs, tags, and object types without checking out or executing repository content.
misconceptions: git show always displays a text patch regardless of object type.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inspect commits, trees, blobs, tags, and object types without checking out or executing repository content.

competencies.statement: Investigate path history, content movement, follow heuristics, and limitations without assuming Git stores rename events.
misconceptions: Git records rename operations as first-class history facts.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Investigate path history, content movement, follow heuristics, and limitations without assuming Git stores rename events.

competencies.statement: Use blame as a line-origin clue and connect it to surrounding commits, review context, and later movement without assigning fault.
misconceptions: The author shown by blame necessarily introduced the current bug.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use blame as a line-origin clue and connect it to surrounding commits, review context, and later movement without assigning fault.

competencies.statement: Create, list, rename, copy, delete, and inspect branches as movable references with upstream metadata.
misconceptions: A branch contains a private copy of every project file.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Create, list, rename, copy, delete, and inspect branches as movable references with upstream metadata.

competencies.statement: Switch branches only after predicting working-tree/index carryover, untracked collisions, and sparse or worktree constraints.
misconceptions: Switching branches always discards uncommitted changes.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Switch branches only after predicting working-tree/index carryover, untracked collisions, and sparse or worktree constraints.

competencies.statement: Find merge bases and explain three-way integration from two tips and their best common ancestor.
misconceptions: A merge compares only the two branch tips.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Find merge bases and explain three-way integration from two tips and their best common ancestor.

competencies.statement: Distinguish fast-forward, true merge, squash, and no-commit integration by topology and team intent.
misconceptions: A squash merge is a merge commit with hidden parents.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish fast-forward, true merge, squash, and no-commit integration by topology and team intent.

competencies.statement: Verify merged tree, combined tests, log topology, diff range, and push target before publishing integration.
misconceptions: No conflict markers means the merged behavior is correct.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Verify merged tree, combined tests, log topology, diff range, and push target before publishing integration.

competencies.statement: Distinguish remote names, URLs, remote-tracking references, local branches, upstreams, and push destinations.
misconceptions: origin is a special central repository that Git discovers automatically.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish remote names, URLs, remote-tracking references, local branches, upstreams, and push destinations.

competencies.statement: Fetch explicit remotes and refspecs, inspect updates, and compare local work before integrating.
misconceptions: git fetch modifies the current branch and working files.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Fetch explicit remotes and refspecs, inspect updates, and compare local work before integrating.

competencies.statement: Explain pull as fetch plus configured integration and select merge, rebase, or fast-forward-only deliberately.
misconceptions: git pull has one universal safe integration behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain pull as fetch plus configured integration and select merge, rebase, or fast-forward-only deliberately.

competencies.statement: Preview source and destination refs, upstream setup, force protections, tags, and deletion semantics before push.
misconceptions: git push always publishes the currently checked-out branch to a branch with the same name.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Preview source and destination refs, upstream setup, force protections, tags, and deletion semantics before push.

competencies.statement: Separate HTTPS or SSH transport, credentials, host trust, authorization, signing, and repository policy.
misconceptions: A successful SSH connection proves permission to push every branch.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate HTTPS or SSH transport, credentials, host trust, authorization, signing, and repository policy.

competencies.statement: Inspect unmerged index stages, ours/theirs/base terminology, paths, and operation context before editing.
misconceptions: Ours always means the feature branch and theirs always means main.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inspect unmerged index stages, ours/theirs/base terminology, paths, and operation context before editing.

competencies.statement: Read conflict markers and diff views while obtaining surrounding base and commit intent.
misconceptions: Choosing both sides line-by-line necessarily preserves both behaviors.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Read conflict markers and diff views while obtaining surrounding base and commit intent.

competencies.statement: Construct a new result from requirements, tests, and both histories rather than treating resolution as text selection.
misconceptions: A syntactically marker-free file is a resolved conflict.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Construct a new result from requirements, tests, and both histories rather than treating resolution as text selection.

competencies.statement: Use continue, skip, quit, and abort controls for merge, rebase, and cherry-pick with operation-specific state awareness.
misconceptions: git abort commands are interchangeable across every in-progress operation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use continue, skip, quit, and abort controls for merge, rebase, and cherry-pick with operation-specific state awareness.

competencies.statement: Record or reuse conflict resolutions only after verifying context, tests, and changed-case behavior.
misconceptions: rerere can safely accept every repeated textual resolution without review.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record or reuse conflict resolutions only after verifying context, tests, and changed-case behavior.

competencies.statement: Trace ignore sources, directory scope, pattern grammar, negation, tracked-state limits, and check-ignore evidence.
misconceptions: Adding a tracked file to .gitignore removes it from history.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace ignore sources, directory scope, pattern grammar, negation, tracked-state limits, and check-ignore evidence.

competencies.statement: Choose repository ignore, info/exclude, and global excludes from whether a rule belongs to every collaborator or one environment.
misconceptions: Personal editor files belong in the project .gitignore by default.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose repository ignore, info/exclude, and global excludes from whether a rule belongs to every collaborator or one environment.

competencies.statement: Use attributes for text normalization, diff, merge, export, and binary behavior with check-attr evidence.
misconceptions: .gitattributes is another spelling of .gitignore.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use attributes for text normalization, diff, merge, export, and binary behavior with check-attr evidence.

competencies.statement: Control repository and working-tree line endings while separating attributes from core.autocrlf and reviewing renormalization.
misconceptions: Line-ending settings change only how diffs are displayed.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Control repository and working-tree line endings while separating attributes from core.autocrlf and reviewing renormalization.

competencies.statement: Prevent and respond to secrets, large binaries, generated artifacts, and sensitive history with policy and rotation boundaries.
misconceptions: Deleting a secret in a later commit makes the exposed credential safe again.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Prevent and respond to secrets, large binaries, generated artifacts, and sensitive history with policy and rotation boundaries.

competencies.statement: Distinguish lightweight and annotated tag objects, messages, taggers, targets, and intended use.
misconceptions: Every tag stores a message and tagger identity.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish lightweight and annotated tag objects, messages, taggers, targets, and intended use.

competencies.statement: Create, inspect, verify, delete, and cautiously replace tags while recognizing immutable-release expectations.
misconceptions: A tag automatically follows later commits on its branch.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Create, inspect, verify, delete, and cautiously replace tags while recognizing immutable-release expectations.

competencies.statement: Apply an explicit version scheme and connect version changes to compatibility, release notes, and source identity.
misconceptions: Semantic version numbers prove that compatibility was tested.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Apply an explicit version scheme and connect version changes to compatibility, release notes, and source identity.

competencies.statement: Record clean tree, exact commit, dependency state, build/test evidence, artifacts, and checksums for a release.
misconceptions: A tag alone makes a build reproducible.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record clean tree, exact commit, dependency state, build/test evidence, artifacts, and checksums for a release.

competencies.statement: Distinguish commit or tag signature validity, signer identity, key trust, account verification, and policy compliance.
misconceptions: A cryptographically valid signature proves the change is safe.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish commit or tag signature validity, signer identity, key trust, account verification, and policy compliance.

competencies.statement: Resolve full and abbreviated object IDs, refs, reflog selectors, ancestry suffixes, and peel operators with ambiguity checks.
misconceptions: HEAD~2 and HEAD^2 always select the same commit.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Resolve full and abbreviated object IDs, refs, reflog selectors, ancestry suffixes, and peel operators with ambiguity checks.

competencies.statement: Interpret two-dot, three-dot, negative revisions, symmetric difference, and command-specific diff behavior.
misconceptions: A..B always means the file changes required to transform A into B.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Interpret two-dot, three-dot, negative revisions, symmetric difference, and command-specific diff behavior.

competencies.statement: Use pathspec magic, exclusions, attributes, top-level anchoring, and separator placement without confusing paths with revisions.
misconceptions: A shell glob and a Git pathspec always select the same paths.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use pathspec magic, exclusions, attributes, top-level anchoring, and separator placement without confusing paths with revisions.

competencies.statement: Search commit messages, changed lines, content occurrences, authors, dates, and paths with pickaxe and regex evidence.
misconceptions: grep over current files can identify the commit that introduced a behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Search commit messages, changed lines, content occurrences, authors, dates, and paths with pickaxe and regex evidence.

competencies.statement: Choose a review range that matches branch intent, merge base, stacked work, and force-push risk.
misconceptions: Comparing two branch tips always isolates only the proposed work.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose a review range that matches branch intent, merge base, stacked work, and force-push risk.

competencies.statement: Select an undo operation from affected layer, publication state, collaboration, desired topology, and recovery evidence.
misconceptions: There is one Git undo command that safely applies to every mistake.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Select an undo operation from affected layer, publication state, collaboration, desired topology, and recovery evidence.

competencies.statement: Use restore for working-tree and index paths with explicit sources, patch selection, and pre-change diffs.
misconceptions: git restore always recovers content from the last commit.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use restore for working-tree and index paths with explicit sources, patch selection, and pre-change diffs.

competencies.statement: Create and verify inverse commits for published changes, merges, and later dependencies without claiming history was erased.
misconceptions: Reverting a merge permanently prevents all its changes from ever returning.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Create and verify inverse commits for published changes, merges, and later dependencies without claiming history was erased.

competencies.statement: Predict reference, index, and working-tree effects of soft, mixed, hard, merge, and keep resets before execution.
misconceptions: git reset --hard is safe when the target commit exists.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Predict reference, index, and working-tree effects of soft, mixed, hard, merge, and keep resets before execution.

competencies.statement: Use reflogs, dangling-object inspection, temporary references, and expiry limits to recover otherwise unreachable work.
misconceptions: Reflog entries are shared remote backups that never expire.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use reflogs, dangling-object inspection, temporary references, and expiry limits to recover otherwise unreachable work.

competencies.statement: Explain rebase as selecting commits, resetting a base, and replaying new commits with new identities.
misconceptions: Rebase moves existing commit objects without changing their IDs.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain rebase as selecting commits, resetting a base, and replaying new commits with new identities.

competencies.statement: Use pick, reword, edit, squash, fixup, drop, exec, and reorder with dependency and test awareness.
misconceptions: Interactive rebase can safely reorder dependent commits without changing behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use pick, reword, edit, squash, fixup, drop, exec, and reorder with dependency and test awareness.

competencies.statement: Limit history rewriting using collaboration state, force-with-lease, recovery refs, and explicit coordination.
misconceptions: A successful force push proves no collaborator work was overwritten.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Limit history rewriting using collaboration state, force-with-lease, recovery refs, and explicit coordination.

competencies.statement: Transfer selected changes while preserving provenance, resolving context, and testing interactions absent from the source branch.
misconceptions: Cherry-pick copies the original commit object unchanged.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Transfer selected changes while preserving provenance, resolving context, and testing interactions absent from the source branch.

competencies.statement: Compare old and rewritten ranges by patch, tree, tests, metadata, and range-diff before replacing a shared reference.
misconceptions: Matching final files proves a rewritten series preserves review meaning.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare old and rewritten ranges by patch, tree, tests, metadata, and range-diff before replacing a shared reference.

competencies.statement: Map client and server hook names to operations, inputs, environment, bypass behavior, and failure effects.
misconceptions: Cloning a repository installs and runs all versioned hooks automatically.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Map client and server hook names to operations, inputs, environment, bypass behavior, and failure effects.

competencies.statement: Keep hook logic fast, deterministic, documented, opt-in where appropriate, and delegated to versioned scripts.
misconceptions: A hook that works in one shell is a portable team gate.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Keep hook logic fast, deterministic, documented, opt-in where appropriate, and delegated to versioned scripts.

competencies.statement: Separate local convenience hooks, CI checks, protected branches, reviews, and server-side enforcement by authority.
misconceptions: A pre-commit hook prevents all invalid commits from reaching a shared repository.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate local convenience hooks, CI checks, protected branches, reviews, and server-side enforcement by authority.

competencies.statement: Treat hooks, filters, diff drivers, submodules, config includes, and build scripts as executable trust boundaries.
misconceptions: Inspecting source files is enough before running repository-provided tooling.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Treat hooks, filters, diff drivers, submodules, config includes, and build scripts as executable trust boundaries.

competencies.statement: Respond to ownership and safe-directory warnings without broadly trusting unrelated repositories.
misconceptions: Adding wildcard safe.directory is the correct fix for every ownership warning.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Respond to ownership and safe-directory warnings without broadly trusting unrelated repositories.

competencies.statement: Choose trunk-based, feature-branch, release-branch, or fork collaboration from delivery, risk, ownership, and release constraints.
misconceptions: One branching model is best for every team and release cadence.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose trunk-based, feature-branch, release-branch, or fork collaboration from delivery, risk, ownership, and release constraints.

competencies.statement: Connect issue context, acceptance criteria, branch scope, commits, tests, and review evidence without making the tracker the source code.
misconceptions: Mentioning an issue number proves the implementation satisfies it.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Connect issue context, acceptance criteria, branch scope, commits, tests, and review evidence without making the tracker the source code.

competencies.statement: Prepare a focused review range, description, changed-case evidence, migration notes, and explicit reviewer questions.
misconceptions: A pull request should include every nearby cleanup to reduce future work.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Prepare a focused review range, description, changed-case evidence, migration notes, and explicit reviewer questions.

competencies.statement: Review behavior, risks, tests, readability, and security with precise evidence while separating required changes from suggestions.
misconceptions: Code review is primarily a style-correction process.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Review behavior, risks, tests, readability, and security with precise evidence while separating required changes from suggestions.

competencies.statement: Apply approvals, required checks, merge queue, update policy, merge method, and branch cleanup consistently.
misconceptions: Passing CI alone grants authority to merge a change.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Apply approvals, required checks, merge queue, update policy, merge method, and branch cleanup consistently.

competencies.statement: Capture repository boundaries, config origins, current operation, worktree/index state, graph, remotes, and recovery refs before repair.
misconceptions: git status alone captures all state needed for repository recovery.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Capture repository boundaries, config origins, current operation, worktree/index state, graph, remotes, and recovery refs before repair.

competencies.statement: Order preservation, reproduction, local repair, tests, history cleanup, and publication from least to most irreversible.
misconceptions: The shortest command sequence is the safest recovery plan.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Order preservation, reproduction, local repair, tests, history cleanup, and publication from least to most irreversible.

competencies.statement: Coordinate rewritten history, conflicts, ownership, release changes, and recovery instructions with explicit affected refs and people.
misconceptions: A force-push warning in chat is sufficient coordination regardless of response.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Coordinate rewritten history, conflicts, ownership, release changes, and recovery instructions with explicit affected refs and people.

competencies.statement: Audit source identity, tree cleanliness, review range, checks, tag, artifact provenance, and remote references before release.
misconceptions: A green release pipeline proves the selected source commit was intended.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Audit source identity, tree cleanliness, review range, checks, tag, artifact provenance, and remote references before release.

competencies.statement: Publish an accessible repository runbook covering setup, workflow, gates, release, recovery, and escalation with tested commands.
misconceptions: Experienced contributors do not need repository-specific recovery documentation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Publish an accessible repository runbook covering setup, workflow, gates, release, recovery, and escalation with tested commands.

credential.title: Git Foundations: Evidence, Collaboration, and Recovery Certificate
